_meta.artifact: NARS-JARVIS — Legal Extraction Validation Corpus (v1.24.0 extraction-pipeline audit)
_meta.purpose: Hand-labeled dense legal clauses for measuring (A) summarize-then-extract vs (B) direct-extract content recall, and (C) the structural expressiveness ceiling of the 4-shape GBNF grammar. Ground truth is the IDEAL logical extraction even where it EXCEEDS the current grammar.

constraint_kinds.property: subject has property/attribute — EXPRESSIBLE as PropertyClaim
constraint_kinds.temporal: deadline / duration / sequence — NOT expressible (needs NAL-7 + bound quantity)
constraint_kinds.modal: shall / must / may — obligation/permission — NOT expressible (no deontic operator)
constraint_kinds.quantitative: amount / percentage / cap bound to a thing — NOT expressible as a bound quantity
constraint_kinds.nary: ≥3 arguments bound together — NOT expressible (binary copula only)

cases.text: Vendor shall notify Customer in writing within seventy-two (72) hours of discovering any data breach affecting Customer Personal Data.

critical_constraints.note: Vendor is the obligated party performing notification
within 72 hours — must bind to the notification action
triggered by discovering a data breach — antecedent of an implication
shall = obligation; loss of this strips legal liability

cases.text: If monthly availability falls below 99.9%, Customer shall be entitled to a service credit equal to 10% of the monthly fee for each full hour of downtime.

critical_constraints.note: consequent: Customer entitled to a service credit
credit = 10% of monthly fee — bound quantity
per full hour of downtime — ties credit to a rate

cases.text: The Receiving Party shall hold all Confidential Information in strict confidence for a period of five (5) years from the date of disclosure and shall not disclose it to any third party.

critical_constraints.note: Receiving Party holds Confidential Information in confidence
clock starts at date of disclosure
shall NOT disclose to third party — negated relation

cases.text: Supplier shall indemnify and hold harmless the Buyer against any third-party claims arising from Supplier's gross negligence or willful misconduct.

critical_constraints.note: only claims ARISING FROM gross negligence/willful misconduct — scoping condition

cases.text: Buyer shall pay all undisputed invoices within thirty (30) days of receipt; overdue amounts shall accrue interest at 1.5% per month.
Either party may terminate this Agreement upon thirty (30) days' written notice if the other party materially breaches and fails to cure such breach within the notice period.

critical_constraints.note: if material breach AND fails to cure
fails to cure within the notice period — nested temporal condition

cases.text: In no event shall either party's aggregate liability exceed the total fees paid by Customer in the twelve (12) months preceding the claim.

critical_constraints.note: cap = total fees paid in prior 12 months — bound quantity

cases.text: Provider shall store and process all Customer Data exclusively within the European Economic Area and shall not transfer such data outside the EEA without Customer's prior written consent.

critical_constraints.note: UNLESS prior written consent — exception condition

cases.text: Customer may audit Vendor's compliance with this Agreement no more than once per calendar year upon at least fourteen (14) days' prior written notice.

critical_constraints.note: max once per calendar year — frequency bound

cases.text: Manufacturer warrants that the Goods will be free from defects in materials and workmanship for ninety (90) days following delivery.

critical_constraints.note: Manufacturer warrants Goods free from defects

cases.text: Processor shall not engage any subprocessor without the Controller's prior written authorization, and shall remain fully liable for the acts and omissions of any approved subprocessor.

critical_constraints.note: Processor remains liable for subprocessor acts

cases.text: Neither party shall be liable for any failure to perform due to events beyond its reasonable control, provided that the affected party notifies the other within five (5) business days of the event.

critical_constraints.note: non-liability IF event beyond reasonable control
party not liable for failure to perform
PROVIDED THAT the affected party gives notice — proviso condition
within 5 business days of the event
